titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.1

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=646 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:30:59.212986+00 | translation moa=647 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:31:00.922816+00

div.p:
Alex’ . dei Gratia Rex Scott’ . Iustic’ . Vicecomitibus . Prepositis . et omnibus probis Hominibus Tocius terre sue Salutem .
Precipio quod fratres nostri de Melros et omnes fratres eiusdem ordinis sint quieti de Teloneo et omnibus aliis consuetudi
nibus et rectitudinibus et de omnibus mercatis que ad suos proprios usus fecerint ubicumque in tota terra
mea uenerint  Sicut Carta domini Regis patris mei Willelmi . testatur . et confirmat . Testibus . Willelmo . de Boscho
Cancellario . meo . Comite Malc’ . de Fif . Willelmo . de Valon’ . Iohanne de Maccuswel’ . Apud Edenb’ . Secundo die . ApriliS .

Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to justices, sheriffs, provosts, and all worthy men of his entire land [sends] greeting. I order that our brethren of Melrose and all brethren of the same order shall be quit of toll and all other customs and what is owed by right even from all trading which they may have done for their own purposes wherever they may have come in the whole of my land, just as the charter of the lord king, William my father, bears witness and affirms. As witnesses: William del Bois, my chancellor; Earl Malcolm of Fife; William de Valognes; John of Maxwell. At Edinburgh on the second day of April.
